medical_history.chief_complaint: Abnormal midtrimester ultrasound showing placental abnormalities
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Referred for targeted sonography at 18 weeks of gestation secondary to a midtrimester ultrasound showing cystic formations within the placenta. Placentomegaly, with cystic formation and dilated fetal bowel, was noted. The antepartum course was complicated by severe preeclampsia requiring delivery by repeat cesarean at 28 weeks of gestation.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Physical findings included epicanthus inversus, prominent interdigital pads, and deep solar creases. Cardiac evaluation showed two small atrial septal defects not requiring repair.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Markedly abnormal (AFP 4.42 MOM, hCG 8.88 MOM, UE3 0.96 MOM, DIA 7.70 MOM)
Normal male karyotype (46,XY) and AF-AFP 0.97 MOM

laboratory_examinations.procedure_name: Cytogenetic study of skin (foreskin from circumcision)
laboratory_examinations.findings: 46,XY (11 cells)/69,XXY (9 cells) mixoploidy on evaluation of 20 cells

radiographic_examinations.findings: Cystic formations within the placenta, placentomegaly, dilated fetal bowel
Placentomegaly with cystic formation and dilated fetal bowel
Placentomegaly (96 mm at widest dimension without cystic structures), mild polyhydramnios, normal targeted fetal anatomy survey

other_tests.findings: Two small atrial septal defects not requiring repair
